name: AI Search
description: Adds an AI search bar to the Jellyfin web client. Ask in natural language and get movies from your own library, personalized to your watch history, backed by a self-hosted AI platform or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
overview: AI natural-language search & recommendations from your own library.

versions.changelog: TV Shows search now works in platform mode: the Movies/TV Shows scope is sent to the platform so TV searches return series and episodes instead of movies (requires the platform's TV/episode semantic index).

The 'Reload models' button on the settings page now refreshes reliably: it shows a loading state, disables while fetching, and bypasses browser caching so newly available models always appear instead of a stale list.

The synopsis length sent to the AI for each candidate is now configurable in the plugin settings (0 = send the full synopsis, no cropping) instead of a fixed 180 characters. Search history now remembers the AI's answer line, so reopening a past search shows it again instead of only the raw prompt, and history titles read better.

Help me choose now hides the recent-searches list while you answer, like a running search. More like this results are now saved into that search's history entry instead of being lost. Episode search is much better: when your query names a series in your library, results are ranked within that series' episodes instead of across the whole library, and the TV retrieval budget was widened.
Fix: Surprise me on TV Shows scope could recommend individual episodes instead of shows. Surprise mode now only picks from series when the TV Shows scope is selected.

History rows can now be deleted individually: a subtle cross fades in on hover (desktop) or is revealed by swiping (mobile), with a full swipe deleting immediately. Surprise me now morphs into a full-width button revealing Movies/TV Shows and Personalize to my taste toggles before running, instead of firing immediately.

Fix: model list dropdown could come back empty even after saving the endpoint URL/key. The Models endpoint now normalizes different provider response shapes (bare array, {data:[...]}, {models:[...]}) and surfaces the real HTTP error in the config page instead of silently showing 'No models returned'.

People queries now work: the model is given each candidate's cast (actors with characters, plus directors) instead of guessing from memory, and only genuine matches are returned. Built against Jellyfin 10.11.0 for 10.11.x compatibility.

Direct mode now builds a local semantic index (embeddings + in-memory nearest-neighbour search) over any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, so results match by meaning instead of sending a catalog slice. Nightly refresh via a scheduled task. Built against Jellyfin 10.11.0 for 10.11.x compatibility.
